suggestions.original_text: A common assumption in current practice is that epistemic honesty is a capability problem: sufficiently capable models, trained with sufficient RLHF, will eventually “know what they know” and report it accurately. Scaling solved grammar; scaling solved coherence [citation]; scaling will solve fabrication. Lin et al.\ tested this assumption directly and found an inverse scaling trend: larger models were less truthful on TruthfulQA [citation]. The theorems that follow explain why this expectation is architecturally incoherent. The constraint is not capability but interface or, in cost terms, the wrong investment tier. Text-only observation lacks the degrees of freedom to verify epistemic state, regardless of what the model internally represents. No amount of capability improvement can escape a verification impossibility: the model may know perfectly well that it is fabricating, but the interface provides no channel through which a supervisor can verify this knowledge independently.
suggestions.suggested_text: Current practice assumes epistemic honesty is a capability problem: that models trained with sufficient RLHF will eventually report what they know accurately. Scaling solved grammar and coherence [citation]; it is expected to solve fabrication. Yet, Lin et al. found an inverse scaling trend: larger models were less truthful on TruthfulQA [citation]. Our theorems show this expectation is architecturally incoherent. The constraint is the interface, not capability. Text-only observation lacks the degrees of freedom to verify epistemic state, regardless of the model's internal representations. This is a verification impossibility: a model may know it is fabricating, but the interface provides no channel for a supervisor to verify this independently.
suggestions.explanation: Replaced wordy introductory phrases, tightened phrasing, and merged the final two clauses for conciseness.

suggestions.original_text: Our argument proceeds in two stages. First, we show that any policy conditioning only on the query cannot satisfy epistemic honesty across ambiguous world states (thm:representational: Representational Impossibility). Second, we show that even if the architecture is expanded to include retrieval, a bounded supervisor cannot provide the training signal needed to learn honest behavior (thm:learnability: Learnability Impossibility). Together, these results establish that epistemic honesty is unachievable for text-only observation models under realistic supervision constraints.

Second, even with retrieval, a bounded supervisor cannot provide the training signal to learn honest behavior (thm:learnability). Together, these results establish that epistemic honesty is unachievable for text-only observation models under realistic supervision constraints.

These results are prompt-agnostic: they hold for any policy that exports only text, regardless of whether a system prompt instructs the model to be “helpful,” “honest,” or otherwise.

suggestions.original_text: In statistical terms, thm:representational states that epistemic honesty is non-identifiable under text-only observation: the mapping from world states to observable distributions is many-to-one. Worlds w_A and w_B induce identical observable distributions (|q) despite requiring different honest behaviors. No amount of data from this observation channel can distinguish them. This connects the impossibility to classical results on observational equivalence in statistics and diagnosability in systems theory [citation]. The theorem formalizes a property of the existing LLM interface, one we did not design, and identifies the precise architectural assumption (conditioning on q alone) whose relaxation enables escape (sec:escape). The result may appear simple in hindsight, but the architecture it describes (query in, text out, no exported logits) matches the dominant LLM-as-a-service interface, and recent API changes have moved to further restrict log-probability access for newer reasoning models. If this impossibility were well-understood, we would expect interface designs to reflect it; instead, the industry is expanding operational observability (traces, spans, latency logs) while narrowing epistemic observability.

suggestions.original_text: Our contribution is a formal impossibility result. Prior work documents symptoms; we formalize one structural cause. Prior work proposes treatments; we explain why they cannot fully resolve the verification problem under text-only observation.
suggestions.suggested_text: We prove a formal impossibility result. Prior work documents symptoms and proposes treatments; we formalize a structural cause and explain why treatments fail under text-only observation.
suggestions.explanation: Combined sentences and replaced wordy phrases ('Our contribution is' -> 'We prove', 'cannot fully resolve the verification problem' -> 'fail') for conciseness.

suggestions.original_text: These findings instantiate our cond:hallucination: in each domain, plausible fabrications are cheap to generate and expensive to verify, placing supervisors in the hallucination regime where gradient signal cannot distinguish truth from fabrication.
suggestions.suggested_text: These findings instantiate our `cond:hallucination`: in each domain, cheap-to-generate, expensive-to-verify fabrications place supervisors where gradient signals cannot distinguish truth from fabrication.
suggestions.explanation: Rephrased to be more direct, using hyphenated modifiers and cutting the redundant phrase 'in the hallucination regime'.

suggestions.original_text: A substantial body of work examines model internal states: transformer circuits [citation], sparse features [citation], Activation Oracles [citation] that train LLMs to interpret other models' internals, and the “Assistant Axis” [citation] showing that internal persona state diverges from text behavior.
suggestions.suggested_text: Much work examines model internals, including transformer circuits [citation], sparse features [citation], Activation Oracles [citation] for interpreting other models' internals, and the “Assistant Axis” [citation] showing internal persona state diverges from text behavior.
suggestions.explanation: Replaced 'A substantial body of work' with 'Much work' and tightened descriptive clauses (e.g., 'that train LLMs to interpret' -> 'for interpreting').

suggestions.original_text: Our work is complementary but narrower: we do not require understanding a model's learned circuits, only that inference-time signals differ measurably between grounded and fabricated generation.
suggestions.suggested_text: Our work is complementary but narrower, requiring not an understanding of learned circuits, but only that inference-time signals measurably differ between grounded and fabricated generation.
suggestions.explanation: Restructured the sentence with 'requiring not... but only...' to be more active and concise.

suggestions.original_text: Our thm:learnability formalizes why: bounded supervisors cannot distinguish plausible fabrications from truthful responses, so the learning signal is systematically corrupted.
suggestions.suggested_text: Our `thm:learnability` formalizes why: bounded supervisors cannot distinguish plausible fabrications from truthful responses, systematically corrupting the learning signal.
suggestions.explanation: Changed the final clause to a participle phrase, which is shorter and more direct.

suggestions.original_text: Calibration methods, conformal prediction [citation], and verbalized uncertainty [citation] attempt to surface model confidence through the text channel.
suggestions.suggested_text: Calibration methods, conformal prediction [citation], and verbalized uncertainty [citation] surface model confidence via the text channel.
suggestions.explanation: Removed the weaker 'attempt to' and replaced 'through' with the more concise 'via'.

suggestions.original_text: This is the most sophisticated text-channel approach to date, partially addressing gaming by measuring agreement between outputs. But it remains text-channel: the clustering operates on generated text, and a bounded supervisor evaluating semantic equivalence remains subject to gaming in the limit.
suggestions.suggested_text: While the most sophisticated text-channel approach, it only partially addresses gaming by measuring output agreement, as clustering on generated text leaves a bounded supervisor subject to gaming.
suggestions.explanation: Merged two sentences, removing redundant phrases ('to date', 'But it remains text-channel', 'in the limit') and tightening the causal link with 'as'.

suggestions.original_text: Our tensor interface extracts signals from the computation that produced a single output, which are the signals the model cannot independently control.
suggestions.suggested_text: Our tensor interface extracts signals from the computation producing an output—signals the model cannot control.
suggestions.explanation: Used a participle phrase ('producing') and an em-dash to create a more concise appositive, and removed redundant words ('that produced a single', 'independently').

suggestions.original_text: Xu et al. [citation] prove that hallucination is inevitable for LLMs over the class of computable functions, which is a computational limit.
suggestions.suggested_text: Xu et al. [citation] prove hallucination is inevitable for LLMs on computable functions, a computational limit.
suggestions.explanation: Removed 'that', shortened 'over the class of' to 'on', and converted the final clause into a more direct appositive.

suggestions.original_text: Our result is orthogonal: we prove that even if a system could be honest, a bounded supervisor observing only text cannot verify that honesty, which is an observational limit.
suggestions.suggested_text: Our result is orthogonal: we prove a bounded, text-only supervisor cannot verify a system's honesty (even if it could be honest), an observational limit.
suggestions.explanation: Rephrased to be more direct ('a bounded supervisor observing only text' -> 'a bounded, text-only supervisor') and converted the final clause to an appositive.

suggestions.original_text: Both are structural, but they constrain different dimensions of system design.
suggestions.suggested_text: Both are structural, constraining different dimensions of system design.
suggestions.explanation: Changed the second clause to a more concise participle phrase.

suggestions.original_text: The Fischer-Lynch-Paterson theorem [citation] proved that consensus is impossible under asynchrony with one faulty process.
suggestions.suggested_text: The Fischer-Lynch-Paterson (FLP) theorem [citation] proved consensus is impossible under asynchrony with one faulty process.
suggestions.explanation: Removed 'that' and introduced the acronym 'FLP' for potential later use, which is standard practice and saves space if used again.

suggestions.original_text: Our result plays an analogous role: text-only observation models with bounded supervision cannot guarantee epistemic honesty, but systems that export structured epistemic state can escape the impossibility class.
suggestions.suggested_text: Our result is analogous: bounded, text-only supervision cannot guarantee epistemic honesty, but systems exporting structured epistemic state can escape this limit.
suggestions.explanation: Shortened phrases ('plays an analogous role' -> 'is analogous', 'text-only observation models with bounded supervision' -> 'bounded, text-only supervision') and replaced 'impossibility class' with the shorter 'limit'.

suggestions.original_text: clark2025epistemicalignmentmediatingframework [citation] note that current interfaces lack structured ways to specify uncertainty or sourcing; we provide the formal impossibility explaining why text-only evaluation cannot suffice.
suggestions.suggested_text: Clark et al. [citation] note current interfaces lack structured ways to specify uncertainty or sourcing; our impossibility result explains why text-only evaluation cannot suffice.
suggestions.explanation: Used standard 'et al.' for the long citation key, removed 'that', and rephrased 'we provide the formal impossibility explaining why' to the more direct 'our impossibility result explains why'.

suggestions.original_text: wright2025predictionstructuringepistemic [citation] propose architectures with explicit propositions and justification graphs, which are closest in spirit to our escape conditions, but frame these as replacements for stochastic generation.
suggestions.suggested_text: Wright et al. [citation] propose architectures with explicit propositions and justification graphs, close to our escape conditions, but as replacements for stochastic generation.
suggestions.explanation: Used 'et al.', and trimmed wordy phrases ('which are closest in spirit to' -> 'close to', 'frame these as' -> 'as').

suggestions.original_text: The gap we fill is formalization: we prove why the problem is structural and identify architectural properties that enable escaping it.
suggestions.suggested_text: We formalize this structural problem and identify architectural escape conditions.
suggestions.explanation: Rewrote the entire sentence to be more active and direct, replacing nominalizations ('formalization') with verbs ('formalize') and tightening descriptions.

Tensor-guided verification improves accuracy for OLMo (69.5% -> 75.1% at 10%), Llama (82.5% -> 88.1%), Qwen (87.0% -> 91.7%), and Mistral (64.0% -> 72.0%). Cross-model entropy agreement on the same queries yields a mean pairwise Spearman's ρ of 0.762, indicating the signal is driven by query properties, not model artifacts.

suggestions.original_text: At 30% budget, the composed judge (91.8%) outperforms the tensor-guided judge alone (90.2%). The advantage is small in aggregate but structurally significant: it comes entirely from citation queries where entropy signals invert. Fabricated citations produce lower entropy than real ones because the model generates plausible fakes more fluently than it retrieves specific bibliographic details. On citation queries, the tensor-guided judge alone underperforms; the bounded-lookup judge catches what entropy misses. This confirms the compositional defense argument: the tensor and the bounded judge have complementary failure modes, and principled composition allocates a finite budget across failure classes rather than spreading it uniformly.

suggestions.original_text: Each row maps an investment strategy to its return. Row 1 (No judge, 75.8%) is the zero-investment baseline. Row 2 (Text-guided, length) uses response word count, a text-channel signal available at zero marginal cost. Row 3 (Tensor-guided) uses per-token entropy, which discriminates among outputs of similar length, reaching cases the text channel cannot. The consistent advantage (+2.5–3.9pp across budget levels) reflects a structural difference (sec:eval_general): text features capture behavioral patterns that vary across models; entropy captures computational uncertainty that generalizes. Row 4 (Composed) demonstrates that tiers compose: different judges cover different failure classes, achieving the highest accuracy at 20% and 30% budget levels.

Row 3 uses per-token entropy, which discriminates where text cannot; its consistent advantage (+2.5–3.9pp) reflects a structural difference between behavioral text features and generalizable computational uncertainty (sec:eval_general). Row 4 shows that composing judges with different failure classes yields the highest accuracy.
suggestions.explanation: Aggressively summarized this paragraph, which recaps table results. Combined clauses and removed repetition of points made elsewhere.

suggestions.original_text: Establishing reliable ground truth is itself a bounded verification problem. Our initial evaluator used substring matching and refusal markers, which is precisely the “text-only bounded supervisor” our theorems describe, and achieved only 68.8% accuracy, exhibiting the predicted failure modes (negation blindness, encoding mismatch, hedged fabrications passing as refusals). The correction required a stratified evaluator combining programmatic verification with LLM-assisted classification: an instance of the escape condition applied to the evaluation pipeline itself. Validated against human review of 80 blinded items, agreement was 75/80 (93.8%), with five characterizable disagreements orthogonal to experimental conditions. Note: the Tier 2 LLM classifier (Qwen3 4B) is also one of the four test models; however, the evaluation task (binary classification of answer correctness) differs from the generation task, and human calibration validates the evaluator independently.
suggestions.suggested_text: Establishing reliable ground truth is a bounded verification problem. Our initial text-only evaluator (a "bounded supervisor" using substring matching) achieved only 68.8% accuracy, showing predicted failure modes like negation blindness. Correction required a stratified evaluator combining programmatic verification with LLM-assisted classification—an instance of the escape condition applied to our evaluation pipeline. Validated against 80 blinded human reviews, agreement was 75/80 (93.8%); the five disagreements were orthogonal to our experimental conditions. The LLM classifier (Qwen3 4B) is one of our test models, but its evaluation task (binary classification) differs from the generation task, and human calibration validates it independently.
suggestions.explanation: Streamlined every sentence by removing conversational phrasing ('is itself', 'which is precisely'), using parentheticals, and converting passive constructions to active ones.

suggestions.section: Confound Analysis: Text Features vs. Tensor Signals
suggestions.original_text: The bounded verification results above show that tensor-guided triage is cost-effective. But a natural objection arises: is the tensor signal merely a proxy for text-channel features? Response length, hedging language, and vocabulary complexity are all observable without a tensor interface. If they discriminate equally well, the tensor interface adds cost without value.
suggestions.suggested_text: Our results show tensor-guided triage is cost-effective, but the tensor signal could be a proxy for text-channel features like response length or hedging language. If such features discriminate equally well, the tensor interface adds cost without value.
suggestions.explanation: Removed conversational lead-ins ('The results above show that', 'a natural objection arises') and combined two sentences to create a more direct setup for the section.

suggestions.section: Confound Analysis: Text Features vs. Tensor Signals
suggestions.original_text: Our primary query set has a systematic asymmetry: knowable queries produce concise factual answers while unknowable queries produce verbose fabrications. We construct a length-controlled evaluation with a 2x2 design: short/long x knowable/unknowable, with 25 queries per cell evaluated on all four architectures (400 total evaluations).

We created a length-controlled 2x2 evaluation (short/long × knowable/unknowable) with 25 queries per cell, run on all four architectures (400 total).
suggestions.explanation: Replaced wordy phrases ('has a systematic asymmetry' -> 'is asymmetric') and used more direct verbs ('construct' -> 'created').

suggestions.section: Confound Analysis: Text Features vs. Tensor Signals
suggestions.original_text: The pattern has two components. First, text features that discriminate for one model fail for others: hedging density achieves 0.837 for OLMo but 0.435 for Mistral; refusal density achieves 0.900 for Qwen but 0.563 for Llama. Each text feature captures a behavioral idiosyncrasy of a particular model's training, not a computational property that generalizes. Second, per-token entropy exceeds 0.85 for three of four models and achieves the highest pooled AUC of any signal tested.
suggestions.suggested_text: Text features that discriminate for one model fail for others: hedging density is 0.837 for OLMo but 0.435 for Mistral; refusal density is 0.900 for Qwen but 0.563 for Llama. Each text feature captures a model-specific behavioral idiosyncrasy, not a generalizable computational property. In contrast, per-token entropy exceeds 0.85 for three of four models and has the highest pooled AUC.
suggestions.explanation: Removed structural lead-ins ('The pattern has two components. First... Second...') and tightened descriptive clauses.

suggestions.section: Confound Analysis: Text Features vs. Tensor Signals
suggestions.original_text: A natural objection is that combining text features might outperform any individual feature. We trained a logistic regression on all six text features. Within-distribution (10-fold cross-validation on the length-controlled data), the combined classifier achieves pooled AUC 0.799, exceeding entropy's 0.757. However, when trained on the primary query set (sec:eval_bounded) and tested on the length-controlled data, the classifier collapses to AUC 0.511, no better than random, while entropy remains at 0.757 without any training data. The combined classifier learned distributional patterns (verbose fabrication, terse facts) that do not transfer when the distribution changes. Per-token entropy requires no labeled data and no distributional assumptions; it measures the computation directly.
suggestions.suggested_text: Combining text features might outperform any single feature. However, when trained on the primary query set (sec:eval_bounded) and tested on length-controlled data, the classifier's AUC collapses to 0.511 (random), while entropy remains at 0.757 without training. The classifier learned distributional patterns (e.g., verbose fabrications) that do not transfer across distributions. Entropy requires no labeled data or distributional assumptions; it measures the computation directly.
suggestions.explanation: Removed conversational framing, combined short sentences, and tightened phrasing throughout the paragraph for conciseness.

suggestions.section: Confound Analysis: Text Features vs. Tensor Signals
suggestions.original_text: This is the impossibility result instantiated empirically. Text-channel features carry behavioral signals that vary across training procedures and query distributions. A model trained to hedge less, refuse differently, or use shorter sentences defeats the corresponding text detector without changing the computation that determines whether it is fabricating. A combined classifier trained on one query distribution collapses on another. Per-token entropy captures a computational property, the model's distributional uncertainty over next tokens, that the model cannot independently control under standard training objectives, the same structural pattern that makes code plagiarism detection (MOSS) robust [citation].
suggestions.suggested_text: This empirically instantiates our impossibility result. Text-channel features carry behavioral signals that vary with training and query distributions. A model trained to change its textual behavior (e.g., hedge less) can defeat a text detector without changing the underlying fabrication computation. A classifier trained on one query distribution collapses on another. In contrast, per-token entropy captures distributional uncertainty, a computational property the model cannot easily control under standard training, similar to the robust signal in MOSS code plagiarism detection [citation].
suggestions.explanation: Rephrased sentences to be more direct and active. Simplified the long final sentence by breaking down the complex description of entropy's properties.

suggestions.section: Confound Analysis: Text Features vs. Tensor Signals
suggestions.original_text: When we test whether entropy predicts correctness (using TruthfulQA [citation] correct vs. incorrect answer pairs), AUC drops to approximately 0.53, which is no better than random. This distinction is central to our claim: the tensor interface escapes the verification impossibility, not the correctness problem. Entropy discriminates epistemic mode, whether the model is retrieving or fabricating, not whether a retrieved answer is right. These are different tasks with different difficulty: mode detection requires distinguishing computational regimes; correctness detection requires ground-truth access that no internal signal can provide. Triage operates on the former: it identifies which outputs to spend verification budget on, not which outputs are correct. The budget curve (tab:budget) demonstrates that entropy-guided triage allocates verification resources more effectively than text-guided triage, even though neither signal predicts correctness directly.
suggestions.suggested_text: Entropy does not predict correctness: on TruthfulQA correct/incorrect answer pairs, its AUC is ~0.53 (random). This distinction is central: the tensor interface escapes verification impossibility, not the correctness problem. Entropy discriminates epistemic mode (retrieving vs. fabricating), not whether a retrieved answer is correct. These tasks differ in difficulty: mode detection distinguishes computational regimes, while correctness requires ground-truth access unavailable to any internal signal. Triage operates on the former, identifying which outputs merit verification, not which are correct. The budget curve (tab:budget) shows entropy-guided triage allocates verification resources more effectively than text-guided triage, though neither signal predicts correctness.
suggestions.explanation: Systematically tightened every sentence in this long paragraph by removing wordy phrases, using parentheticals for conciseness, and choosing more direct verbs.
